guide-open-orders.title: Guide — Open Orders
guide-open-orders.description: Simplified view of business context, endpoint usage, and payment-lifecycle dependencies.

sections.title: Business context
sections.content: Open Orders is designed for partners that need operational ownership of order fulfillment while maintaining event consistency with the platform.

sections.title: When to use Open Orders endpoints

items.title: Order intake
items.text: Use webhook/polling + acknowledge to guarantee reliable ingestion.

items.title: Operational flow
items.text: Use picking/invoice/courier endpoints when retailer-driven events are required.

items.title: Exception handling
items.text: Use cancellation/substitutions with traceability and idempotency.

sections.title: Payment lifecycle dependency
sections.content: Order state transitions must stay consistent with payment/fiscal lifecycle checkpoints to avoid reconciliation drift.

items.title: Ingest
items.text: Webhook/Polling + immediate acknowledge.

items.title: Process
items.text: Picking/invoice updates per operation model.

items.title: Dispatch
items.text: Request courier and follow delivery events.

items.title: Close loop
items.text: Handle errors/cancellations with idempotent logs.

endpoints-overview.title: Endpoints — recipes and examples
endpoints-overview.description: Quick map across request recipes, response examples, and copyable snippets.

open-orders-intro.title: What is Open Orders?
open-orders-intro.description: Open Orders enables retailers to receive orders placed by customers on the Rappi platform and manage fulfillment using their own picking and logistics infrastructure.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The system integrates new orders directly into the retailer's ERP/OMS and manages event notifications between Rappi and the retailer throughout the entire order lifecycle.

This allows retailers to **maximize team productivity** during low-demand periods, using external channels (like Rappi) to generate additional volume without operational overload.

sections.title: Implementation Commitment
sections.content: Every new feature released by the Open Orders team improves the user experience in integrated stores. As an Open Orders retailer, **you must commit to implementing new features within 8 weeks** of release.

items.title: Active Inventory Integration
items.text: You already have a functional stock/price integration with Rappi.

items.title: Robust stock management
items.text: Your system prevents stockouts that would cause order cancellations.

items.title: Multi-channel ERP/POS capacity
items.text: Your management system can absorb external channel orders without impacting local operations.

items.title: Team trained for agile fulfillment
items.text: Your team is prepared to fulfill orders with strict picking time SLAs.

items.title: Development resources available
items.text: You have access to a technical team or integration provider to implement and maintain the connection.

items.title: Understand the order lifecycle
items.text: Map all possible events and statuses. Understand how each transition impacts the customer experience.

items.title: Map internal events to Rappi
items.text: Identify how your OMS/ERP events correspond to events expected by the Rappi platform.

items.title: Identify system limitations
items.text: Assess whether your ERP or picking system has restrictions impacting the implementation of any event.

items.title: Develop webhooks
items.text: Implement endpoints to receive order events sent by Rappi to your server.

items.title: Notify all fulfillment events
items.text: Ensure that **all** fulfillment events are notified to Rappi in real time, without exception.

modos-operacao.title: Modes of Operation
modos-operacao.description: Open Orders offers two integration modes. The choice depends on who controls the picking process and who initiates status updates.

sections.title: Decide before implementing
sections.content: The operation mode determines the entire integration architecture. Define this together with your Rappi Integrations Specialist before starting development.

items.title: Orders Lite
items.text: Implements only Rappi Events. Status updates (picking start, invoices) are Rappi-initiated. Compatible with Rappi's picking solutions.

items.title: Orders Full
items.text: Implements both Rappi Events and Retailer Events. The retailer initiates status updates. Incompatible with Rappi's picking solutions.

lite-fluxo.title: Orders Lite — flow
lite-fluxo.description: Lite focuses on Rappi-originated events; status transitions follow the model compatible with Rappi picking.

sections.title: Summary
sections.content: **Orders Lite** centers on **Rappi events**. See also **Receiving data → Webhook, polling & mode**.

items.title: Order created
items.text: Your system receives notifications (e.g. webhook).

items.title: Rappi events
items.text: Consume events as agreed with your integration contact.

items.title: Completion
items.text: Follow the lifecycle documented under **Order Lifecycle**.

full-fluxo.title: Orders Full — flow
full-fluxo.description: Full mode adds retailer-initiated fulfillment events; not compatible with Rappi picking solutions.

sections.title: Not compatible with Rappi picking
sections.content: **Orders Full** requires your system to initiate and notify retailer events as contractually defined.

items.title: Receive Rappi events
items.text: order_created, courier_assigned, etc.

items.title: Publish retailer events
items.text: start_picking, invoice, request_courier from your OMS to the API.

items.title: Keep parity
items.text: Operational steps must map to expected platform events.

recebimento-visao-geral.title: Webhook, polling & mode (Lite/Full)
recebimento-visao-geral.description: How order data reaches your system and how it relates to Lite vs Full.

sections.content: You may use **webhooks** (push) and/or **polling** (pull) depending on your technical design and mode. There is no single pattern for every retailer.

items.title: Webhook
items.text: Platform sends HTTP callbacks to your endpoints.

items.title: Polling
items.text: Your system queries the API on an interval for pending orders/events.

sections.title: Next steps
sections.content: See **Via Webhook**, **Via Polling**, and **Acknowledge Order** for technical detail.

autenticacao.title: Authentication
autenticacao.description: All Rappi API calls require authentication via OAuth 2.0 with the Client Credentials flow. Access tokens are obtained using credentials provided by Rappi during onboarding.

sections.title: Partner Credentials
sections.content: The `client_id` and `client_secret` are provided by the Rappi integrations team during the onboarding process. Never share these credentials or expose them in client-side/frontend code.

sections.title: Token Expiration
sections.content: The token expires in **3600 seconds** (1 hour). Implement automatic renewal before expiration to avoid interruptions in order reception.

lifecycle.title: Understanding the Order Lifecycle
lifecycle.description: Orders flow through a sequence of events. The system recognizes two event types: Rappi Events (platform-generated) and Retailer Events (OMS or picking solution-generated).

sections.title: Two types of events
sections.content: **Rappi Events** are generated and sent by the platform to your system. **Retailer Events** are initiated by your system and sent to the Rappi API. In **Lite** mode, only Rappi Events are implemented.

rows.description: Order created by customer. First notification sent to retailer with all order details.
Retailer notifies that store staff has started picking items.
Picking complete. Retailer sends the final list of picked items and invoice amount.
Retailer requests Rappi to dispatch a courier to collect the order from the store.
Rappi confirms a courier has been assigned and is heading to the store.
Order delivered to customer. Final event in the normal lifecycle. Includes `delivered_at` timestamp.

items.title: Webhook (Recommended)
items.text: Rappi makes POST requests to your endpoint immediately on order creation. Minimum latency, ideal for fast confirmation.

items.title: Polling
items.text: Your system periodically queries the GET endpoint to retrieve unacknowledged events. Acts as a safety fallback.

status-pedido.title: Order Status
status-pedido.description: Statuses represent the current state of an order in the Rappi system. Each event transition updates the order status.

rows.description: Order received and confirmed. Ready to start picking.
Retailer reported the invoice. Final basket confirmed, awaiting courier.
Order successfully delivered. Final state of the normal lifecycle.
Cancelled by Rappi, retailer, or timeout. Final state.

eventos-rappi.title: Rappi Events
eventos-rappi.description: Events generated by the Rappi platform and sent to the retailer's system. Your server must be prepared to receive and process these events in real time.

sections.title: Applies to both modes
sections.content: All Rappi Events must be implemented in both **Lite** and **Full** modes.

sections.title: order_created
sections.content: New order notification. This is the most critical event — your system must process and confirm receipt as quickly as possible.

sections.title: Payload — order_created
sections.code:
{
  "event_type": "order_created",
  "order_id": "RAP-123456789",
  "store_id": "store_abc123",
  "created_at": "2024-03-15T14:32:10Z",
  "customer": {
    "name": "John Smith",
    "phone": "+1 555 123-4567",
    "address": {
      "street": "123 Main St",
      "city": "Miami",
      "state": "FL",
      "zipcode": "33101",
      "latitude": 25.7617,
      "longitude": -80.1918
    }
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "sku": "SKU-001234",
      "product_id": "prod_001",
      "name": "Organic Whole Milk 1L",
      "quantity": 3,
      "unit_price": 350,
      "total_price": 1050,
      "unit": "UN",
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "sku": "SKU-005678",
      "product_id": "prod_002",
      "name": "Instant Coffee 200g",
      "quantity": 1,
      "unit_price": 899,
      "total_price": 899,
      "unit": "UN",
      "notes": "Prefer sealed packaging"
    }
  ],
  "subtotal": 1949,
  "total_amount": 1949,
  "currency": "USD",
  "payment": {
    "method": "IN_APP",
    "status": "PAID"
  }
}

sections.title: courier_assigned
sections.content: Rappi has assigned a courier who is heading to the store to collect the order. Use this event to prepare the order for pickup.

sections.title: order_finished
sections.content: The courier confirmed delivery to the customer. This is the final event in the normal order lifecycle.

eventos-varejista.title: Retailer Events
eventos-varejista.description: Events initiated by the retailer's system and sent to the Rappi API. Applies only to Orders Full mode.

sections.title: Orders Full only
sections.content: Retailer events are only implemented in **Orders Full** mode. In Lite mode, these events are managed automatically by Rappi.

rows.description: Notifies that store staff has started picking items.
Sends the final list of picked items and invoice amount.
Requests dispatch of a courier to collect the order.

sections.title: Execution order
sections.content: Retailer events must be sent **in the correct order**: `start_picking` → `report_invoice` → `request_courier`. Sending out of order will return a `422` error.

via-webhook.title: Receiving Orders via Webhook
via-webhook.description: The recommended method for receiving orders. Rappi makes a POST request to your registered endpoint immediately after an order is created.

sections.title: Recommended Method
sections.content: Use webhooks to ensure the **lowest possible latency** in receiving orders. Combine with polling as a safety fallback.

items.title: Create your endpoint
items.text: Implement an HTTPS endpoint on your server that accepts `POST` requests. Must respond `HTTP 200` within **5 seconds**.

items.title: Register the URL
items.text: Provide your endpoint URL to the Rappi integrations team during onboarding. The URL must be publicly accessible.

items.title: Process asynchronously
items.text: When receiving the webhook, return `200` immediately and process the payload in the background to avoid timeouts.

items.title: Implement idempotency
items.text: Use the `order_id` as an idempotency key to avoid duplicate processing in case of retries.

sections.title: Webhook Handler (Node.js)

via-polling.title: Receiving Orders via Polling
via-polling.description: Alternative or complementary method to webhooks. Your system periodically queries the endpoint to retrieve unacknowledged order events.

sections.title: Use as Fallback
sections.content: For most retailers, we recommend using **both**: webhook for low latency + polling as a safety fallback to ensure no orders are missed.

rows.name: store_id
rows.description: Rappi store ID. Provided during onboarding.

acusar-recebimento.title: Acknowledge Order
acusar-recebimento.description: Confirms to the Rappi system that the order has been received by the retailer. This step is required for the order to advance in the lifecycle.

sections.title: Confirm immediately
sections.content: Orders not confirmed within the SLA may be automatically cancelled. Implement acknowledge as the **first action** after receiving `order_created`.

iniciar-separacao.title: Start Picking
iniciar-separacao.description: Notifies Rappi that the store staff has started picking the order items. Orders Full only.

sections.title: Orders Full only
sections.content: This endpoint is exclusive to **Orders Full** mode. In Lite mode, the picking start event is managed internally by Rappi.

informar-invoice.title: Report Invoice
informar-invoice.description: Notifies Rappi that picking is complete. Sends the final list of actually picked items and the invoice amount.

sections.title: Final basket vs original order
sections.content: The `report_invoice` represents the **actual final basket** picked by the team. If an item was unavailable or substituted, report that here. The customer will be notified of differences.

solicitar-courier.title: Request Courier
solicitar-courier.description: Requests Rappi to dispatch a courier to collect the order from the store. The order must be in invoice_reported status.

sections.title: Prerequisite
sections.content: `request_courier` must be called **only after** `report_invoice`. The order must be in `invoice_reported` status.

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X POST \
  "https://microservices.rappi.com/api/rocket-retailer/v2/restaurants/store_abc123/orders/RAP-123456789/request-courier" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "ready_at": "2024-03-15T15:05:00Z",
    "pickup_notes": "Order on shelf B, red bag"
  }'

sections.title: After the request
sections.content: When a courier is assigned, you will receive the `courier_assigned` event on your webhook with the courier's details and estimated arrival time.

cancelamento.title: Cancel Order
cancelamento.description: Cancels an active order. Possible in created and invoice_reported statuses. Orders with a courier already assigned cannot be cancelled by the retailer.

sections.title: Customer impact
sections.content: Cancellations directly impact the customer experience. **Avoid cancellations** — invest in accurate stock management to prevent stockouts. High cancellation rates may result in contractual penalties.

rows.name: description
rows.description: Detailed description (max 500 characters). Helps in root cause analysis.

sections.title: Request Example
sections.code:
curl -X POST \
  "https://microservices.rappi.com/api/rocket-retailer/v2/restaurants/store_abc123/orders/RAP-123456789/cancel" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"reason": "OUT_OF_STOCK", "description": "Organic Whole Milk out of stock."}'

substituicoes.title: Item Substitutions
substituicoes.description: When an order item is unavailable, the retailer can report a substitute product in the report_invoice, instead of cancelling the order.

sections.title: Prefer substitutions over cancellations
sections.content: Substitutions preserve the customer experience and avoid cancellations. Whenever possible, offer an equivalent product instead of removing the item.

sections.title: Substitution in report_invoice

erros.title: Error Codes
erros.description: Complete reference of errors returned by the Open Orders API and handling guidance.

sections.title: Standard Error Format
sections.code:
{
  "error": "order_not_found",
  "message": "Order RAP-123456789 not found for store store_abc123.",
  "details": null,
  "request_id": "req_abc123xyz",
  "timestamp": "2024-03-15T14:32:10Z"
}

rows.description: Invalid parameters. Check body and query params.
Missing, invalid or expired token. Renew access_token.
Valid token but no permission for this store or operation.
Invalid status transition or business rule violated.
Rate limit reached (100 req/min). Adjust polling interval.
Rappi internal error. Include `request_id` when reporting to support.
Service temporarily unavailable. Use retry with exponential backoff.
